Notes: Flash point by Tag closed cup - high temperature range
Scope: Flammable liquids with flash point above 4°C; NOT for use with residual fuel oils or liquids with suspended solids

Notes: Flash point by Tag closed cup - low temperature range
Scope: Low-viscosity petroleum distillates; precision is formula-based and depends on avg temperature

Notes: Flash point by Pensky-Martens closed cup - Procedure A
Conc_Range: All flash point ranges applicable to procedure
Scope: Referee method for D975 diesel; distillate fuels including diesel and jet fuel

Notes: Flash point by Pensky-Martens closed cup - Procedure B other types
Sample_Matrix: Residual fuel oils / cutback asphalts / used oils
Scope: Non-distillate petroleum products including residual fuel oils and used lubricating oils

Notes: Flash point by Pensky-Martens closed cup - Procedure B residual fuel oil
Scope: Residual fuel oils specifically; tighter precision than other types at same temperature

Notes: Flash point by Pensky-Martens closed cup - Procedure C
Scope: Biodiesel and biodiesel blends; higher r/R reflects matrix variability

Notes: Pour point of petroleum products - lubricating oil
Scope: Petroleum products that flow under gravity; results rounded to nearest 3°C increment

Notes: Pour point of petroleum products - middle distillate and residual fuel
Sample_Matrix: Middle distillate fuels / residual fuel oils
Scope: D975 diesel pour point guidance; results rounded to nearest 3°C increment

Notes: Kinematic viscosity at 40°C - diesel fuel
Scope: Referee method for D975 viscosity; precision formula-based on average viscosity value

Notes: Kinematic viscosity at 50°C - fuel oils
Scope: Fuel oils per D396; test temperature 50°C; precision formula-based

Notes: Kinematic viscosity at -20°C - jet fuel
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1)
Scope: D1655 viscosity requirement; test at -20°C; wider precision due to low temperature effects

Notes: Smoke point of kerosene and aviation turbine fuel - automated
Sample_Matrix: Kerosene / aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1)
Scope: D1655 requirement; automated method; lower r/R than manual due to reduced operator variability

Notes: Smoke point of kerosene and aviation turbine fuel - manual
Sample_Matrix: Kerosene / aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1)
Scope: D1655 requirement; manual wick method; higher r/R reflects operator variability in wick trimming and flame reading

Notes: Naphthalene hydrocarbons in aviation turbine fuel by UV
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1)
Scope: D1655 requirement max 3.0% vol; UV spectrophotometry at 285 nm; single-component naphthalene proxy

Notes: Freezing point of aviation fuels - visual method
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1 / JP-8)
Scope: D1655 requirement (Jet A max -40°C / Jet A-1 max -47°C); crystal appearance/disappearance method

Notes: Cloud point of petroleum products and liquid fuels - biodiesel blends
Scope: D975 alternative for biodiesel blends; results rounded to nearest 1°C

Notes: Cloud point of petroleum products and liquid fuels - diesel fuels
Scope: D975 cloud point; visual wax crystal appearance; results rounded to nearest 1°C

Notes: Sulfur in petroleum products by wavelength dispersive XRF
Sample_Matrix: Middle distillate fuels / lubricating oils / residual fuels
Scope: D975 referee sulfur method for higher sulfur grades; NOT suitable for <15 ppm (use D5453 or D7039 for ULSD/Tier 3)

Notes: Electrical conductivity of aviation and distillate fuels
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel / jet fuel / distillate fuels
Conc_Range: 1 to 600 pS/m (D1655 requires 50-600 pS/m with static dissipator)
Scope: D1655 conductivity requirement; measures static dissipator additive effectiveness; Emcee model specific precision

Notes: Mercaptans in natural gas and gasoline by potentiometric titration
Scope: D4814 mercaptan sweetness requirement; formula-based precision; NOT for total sulfur

Notes: JFTOT thermal oxidation stability - result is pass/fail tube rating only
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1)
Scope: D1655 requirement; heater tube deposit rating ≤3 and pressure differential ≤25 mmHg; no r/R applicable - binary result

Method: D4052-22 API (Distillates Basestocks and Lube Oil)
Notes: Density / relative density / API gravity by digital density meter
Sample_Matrix: Distillate fuels / base stocks / lube oils
Scope: D975/D1655 density; digital oscillating U-tube; preferred over D1298 for precision; temperature controlled at 15°C

Notes: Density / relative density / API gravity by digital density meter - gasoline
Scope: D4814 density; wider precision for gasoline due to higher volatility and vapor pressure; sample handling critical

Method: D4052-22 Density (Distillates Basestocks and Lube Oil)
Notes: Density by digital density meter - distillates
Sample_Matrix: Distillate fuels / base stocks / lube oils
Scope: D975/D1655 density in g/mL units; same method as API version; report at 15°C

Notes: Density by digital density meter - gasoline
Scope: D4814 density in g/mL; wider precision for gasoline matrix

Notes: Sulfur in petroleum products by energy dispersive XRF - mass% range
Sample_Matrix: Petroleum products including diesel / fuel oils / lube oils
Scope: Energy dispersive XRF; lower precision than WDXRF (D2622); NOT suitable for trace sulfur <0.05%; use for higher sulfur grades

Notes: Sulfur in petroleum products by energy dispersive XRF - trace mg/kg range
Scope: Trace sulfur range; precision degrades at very low concentrations; consider D5453 or D7039 for <50 ppm regulatory work

Notes: Vapor-liquid ratio temperature of fuels
Scope: D4814 vapor lock index; measures temperature at which V/L ratio = 20; related to hot fuel handling

Notes: Dry vapor pressure equivalent (DVPE) - 1 L chamber
Conc_Range: 35 to 100 kPa typical (D4814 limits vary by season/class)
Scope: D4814 RVP surrogate; 1 L sample chamber; DVPE correlates to Reid VP; EPA and D4814 volatility compliance

Notes: Dry vapor pressure equivalent (DVPE) - 250 mL chamber
Scope: D4814 RVP; 250 mL sample chamber variant; slightly wider precision than 1 L chamber

Notes: Cloud point by rapid detection method - biodiesel fuels
Scope: D975 alternative cloud point for biodiesel; automated rapid cooling; results rounded to nearest 1°C

Notes: Cloud point by rapid detection method - general fuels
Scope: D975 cloud point alternative; rapid automated detection; results rounded to nearest 1°C

Notes: Pour point by automatic pressure pulsing - 1°C interval Model 70
Scope: Automated pour point; 1°C step interval; Model 70 instrument specific precision; D975 and lube oil use

Notes: Pour point by automatic pressure pulsing - 1°C interval Models 30/50/70
Scope: Automated pour point; 1°C step; applies to multiple instrument models; D975 acceptable alternative

Notes: Pour point by automatic pressure pulsing - 3°C interval Model 70
Scope: Automated pour point; 3°C step interval; wider precision matches the coarser step size

Notes: Pour point by automatic pressure pulsing - 3°C interval Models 30/50/70
Scope: Automated pour point; 3°C step; applies to multiple instrument models

Notes: Freezing point of aviation fuels by automatic phase transition
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1)
Scope: D1655 alternative to D2386; automated cooling/heating cycle; phase transition detection; same pass/fail limits apply

Notes: Cold filter plugging point (CFPP) of diesel and heating fuels
Scope: D975 low temperature operability; European origin (EN 116 equivalent); plugging of fuel filter at low temperature

Notes: Distillation IBP - gasoline
Scope: D4814 distillation; initial boiling point; ⚠️ r/R are representative - actual values are tabular in D86 Table A1.1 by temperature range

Notes: Distillation 10% recovered - gasoline
Scope: D4814 T10; controls cold start and warm-up; ⚠️ verify r/R against D86 Table A1.1 for your typical T10 range

Notes: Distillation 50% recovered - gasoline
Scope: D4814 T50 / driveability index input; ⚠️ verify r/R against D86 Table A1.1 for your typical T50 range

Notes: Distillation 90% recovered - gasoline
Scope: D4814 T90 / driveability index input; ⚠️ verify r/R against D86 Table A1.1 for your typical T90 range

Notes: Distillation final boiling point - gasoline
Scope: D4814 endpoint limit; ⚠️ verify r/R against D86 Table A1.1; FBP has highest variability in the method

Notes: Distillation IBP - diesel fuel
Scope: D975 distillation; ⚠️ r/R are representative midpoints - verify against D86 Table A1.1 for your temperature range

Notes: Distillation 10% recovered - diesel and jet fuel
Sample_Matrix: Diesel fuel / aviation turbine fuel (Jet A)
Scope: D975 and D1655 distillation; ⚠️ verify r/R against D86 Table A1.1; D1655 has no T10 limit but used for characterization

Notes: Distillation 50% recovered - diesel and jet fuel
Sample_Matrix: Diesel fuel / aviation turbine fuel (Jet A)
Scope: D975 and D1655 T50; ⚠️ verify r/R against D86 Table A1.1 for your temperature range

Notes: Distillation 90% recovered - diesel and jet fuel
Sample_Matrix: Diesel fuel No. 2-D / aviation turbine fuel
Scope: D975 T90 specification limit 282–338°C; D1655 T90 used for characterization; ⚠️ verify r/R against D86 Table A1.1

Notes: Distillation final boiling point - diesel and jet fuel
Scope: D975 and D1655 endpoint; highest variability cut point; ⚠️ verify r/R against D86 Table A1.1; no D975 FBP limit but used for characterization

Notes: Reid vapor pressure - gasoline
Scope: D4814 RVP; traditional wet method; summer/winter volatility class compliance; being superseded by D5191 in many labs

Notes: Reid vapor pressure - aviation fuel
Scope: Aviation gasoline RVP; tighter precision than automotive gasoline; lower vapor pressure range

Notes: Density / relative density / API gravity by hydrometer
Sample_Matrix: All petroleum liquids including gasoline / diesel / jet
Scope: Traditional hydrometer method; referee for D4814/D975/D1655 density; lower precision than digital D4052; temperature correction to 15°C required

Notes: Density by hydrometer - g/mL units
Sample_Matrix: All petroleum liquids including gasoline / diesel / jet
Scope: Traditional hydrometer method in g/mL; same method as API version; temperature correction to 15°C required

Notes: Research octane number of spark-ignition engine fuel
Sample_Matrix: Gasoline / oxygenated gasoline blends (E0 to E85)
Scope: D4814 anti-knock requirement; CFR engine test at 600 rpm; half of anti-knock index (AKI) reported at pump

Notes: Motor octane number of spark-ignition engine fuel
Sample_Matrix: Gasoline / oxygenated gasoline blends (E0 to E85)
Scope: D4814 anti-knock requirement; CFR engine test at 900 rpm; more severe than RON; AKI = (RON+MON)/2

Notes: Cetane number of diesel fuel oil
Scope: D975 ignition quality; CFR engine test; R=2.8 is relatively wide - consider D6890 IQT for better precision at same concentration

Notes: Calculated cetane index - 4-variable equation
Scope: D975 cetane index; NOT valid for biodiesel blends / additized fuel / synthetic fuel; calculated from D86 + D4052 inputs

Notes: Calculated cetane index - 2-variable equation
Scope: D975 alternative cetane index; 2-variable (density + T50 only); less precise than D4737; NOT valid for biodiesel or additized fuel

Notes: Hydrocarbon types by FIA - aromatics
Scope: D975 aromaticity ≤35% vol; D4814 and D1655 characterization; FIA column separation; total aromatic hydrocarbons

Notes: Hydrocarbon types by FIA - olefins
Scope: D4814 olefin content; important for gum/oxidation stability; NOT typically reported for diesel or jet fuel

Notes: Hydrocarbon types by FIA - saturates
Scope: Saturates = 100% - (aromatics + olefins); not directly specified in D975/D1655 but used for fuel characterization

Notes: Sulfur in petroleum products by UV fluorescence - low concentration
Sample_Matrix: Gasoline / diesel / jet fuel / kerosene / ethanol blends
Scope: Preferred method for EPA Tier 3 gasoline (≤10 ppm) and ULSD (≤15 ppm); UV fluorescence; formula-based precision; most common low-sulfur compliance method

Notes: Sulfur in petroleum products by UV fluorescence - higher concentration
Scope: Higher concentration range of D5453; D975 S500 and S5000 grades; different precision formula above 400 ppm threshold

Notes: Copper strip corrosion - qualitative rating result (1A/1B/2A/2B/3/4) - no r/R applicable
Sample_Matrix: Gasoline / diesel / jet fuel / kerosene / lube oils
Scope: D975 / D1655 / D4814 corrosion requirement; 3 hour test at 50°C; result is descriptive rating not numeric - use visual comparison strips

Notes: Ramsbottom carbon residue of petroleum products
Conc_Range: 0.01 to 10 % mass; D975 max 0.35% mass (10% residue)
Scope: D975 carbon residue on 10% residue; indicator of combustion deposit tendency; NOT the same as Conradson carbon (D189)

Notes: Ash content of petroleum products
Sample_Matrix: Diesel fuel / residual fuel oils / crude oils
Scope: D975 ash requirement; measures inorganic mineral content after combustion; low values near detection limit - consider method sensitivity at D975 limits

Notes: Water and sediment in middle distillate fuels by centrifuge
Sample_Matrix: Middle distillate fuels (diesel No. 1-D and No. 2-D)
Scope: D975 referee water and sediment; centrifuge method; low concentrations near reporting limit require careful technique

Notes: Lubricity by high frequency reciprocating rig (HFRR) at 60°C
Conc_Range: 300 to 600 µm wear scar diameter typical; D975 max 520 µm
Scope: D975 lubricity; HFRR wear scar diameter at 60°C; wide r/R (±36 µm) relative to D975 limit; careful sample handling required

Notes: Estimation of net heat of combustion of aviation fuels
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1 / JP-8)
Scope: D1655 net heat of combustion requirement (min 42.8 MJ/kg); estimated from aniline point and density; formula-based precision

Notes: Acidity in aviation turbine fuel
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1)
Scope: D1655 total acidity requirement; potentiometric titration; very low concentrations - technique and titrant freshness critical

Notes: Water separation characteristics by portable separometer
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel (Jet A / Jet A-1) with and without SDA
Conc_Range: 70 to 100 MSEP rating; D1655 min 85 without SDA / min 70 with SDA
Scope: D1655 water separation; measures fuel-water interface quality; additive effects significant; result is index not physical unit

Notes: Gum content in fuels by jet evaporation
Conc_Range: 0 to 15 mg/100mL typical; D4814 max 5 / D1655 max 7
Scope: D4814 and D1655 gum requirement; measures non-volatile residue after evaporation; storage stability indicator

Notes: Free water and particulate contamination - visual inspection
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel / distillate fuels at point of use
Scope: D1655 field check; visual clarity and appearance; Procedure A (unaided eye) / Procedure B (white bucket); no r/R - qualitative result

Notes: Mercaptans in gasoline - potentiometric titration
Scope: D4814 mercaptan sulfur sweetness; NOT total sulfur; formula-based precision at very low concentrations; sample must be free of H2S

Notes: Dry vapor pressure equivalent (DVPE) of gasoline
Conc_Range: 35 to 100 kPa; D4814 limits vary by volatility class and season
Scope: D4814 primary volatility method; DVPE ≈ RVP - 1.8 kPa for E10; EPA 40 CFR 80 compliance testing; 1 L chamber standard

Notes: Water in petroleum products by coulometric Karl Fischer
Sample_Matrix: Aviation turbine fuel / diesel / distillate fuels
Scope: D1655 water content; coulometric titration; formula-based precision; NOT for free water - dissolved water only; sample must be homogeneous

Notes: EPA Tier 3 gasoline sulfur ≤10 ppm - uses D5453 precision
Sample_Matrix: Gasoline including RFG and CBOB at covered refineries and importers
Scope: EPA 40 CFR Part 80 Subpart E; applicable to covered refiners/importers; uses D5453 as primary test method; 10 ppm annual average / 80 ppm per-gallon cap

Notes: EPA ULSD diesel sulfur ≤15 ppm - uses D5453 precision
Sample_Matrix: Ultra-low sulfur diesel (highway / non-road / locomotive / marine)
Scope: EPA 40 CFR Part 80; highway ULSD ≤15 ppm; non-road ULSD ≤15 ppm; uses D5453 / D7039 / D2622 as approved test methods

Notes: Benzene content in gasoline per EPA CAA
Conc_Range: 0.00 to 1.50 % vol; EPA max 0.62% annual average
Scope: EPA 40 CFR Part 80 Subpart F; uses D5580 GC method; annual average 0.62% vol / per-gallon cap 1.30% vol; RFG areas have stricter limits

Notes: EPA volatility program RVP - uses D5191 precision
Sample_Matrix: Gasoline sold in ozone nonattainment areas (summer)
Conc_Range: 54 to 68 kPa (Class B/C summer limits); 9.0 psi max typical
Scope: EPA CAA Section 211(h) volatility program; D5191 DVPE is approved test method; summer (June-Sept) limits vary by area classification

Notes: Benzene in finished gasoline by GC
Scope: EPA primary benzene test method; gas chromatography separation; formula-based precision at trace levels critical for compliance

Notes: Toluene in finished gasoline by GC
Scope: D4814 characterization; GC method same run as benzene; no specification limit for toluene directly but contributes to aromatics total

Notes: Total aromatics in finished gasoline by GC
Scope: D4814 aromatics characterization; sum of benzene + toluene + xylenes + C9+ from GC; wider precision at higher concentrations

Notes: Sulfur in gasoline and diesel by MWDXRF - low concentration
Scope: Best precision of XRF methods at low levels; EPA-approved for Tier 3 and ULSD compliance; monochromatic WDXRF; preferred over D2622 and D5453 for <15 ppm regulatory work per PTP data

Notes: Sulfur in gasoline and diesel by MWDXRF - higher concentration
Scope: Higher concentration range of D7039; still better precision than D2622 at same concentration; D975 S500 grade monitoring
